column1: # Edit the value column only. Blank value = keep the built-in default. Write 'none' to empty a list. Unknown keys are rejected.

column5: description
Monthly savings ambition in Rs. Gate C6 reports MEETS/BELOW against this. A verdict only — a smaller plan still executes.
Headline discount % the brand is steering toward (dashboard KPI).
Revenue-weighted portfolio discount % the flywheel glides toward.
Quarter label the discount target is set for, e.g. Q4 2026.
Weekly discount-spend cap as a fraction of gross sales. 0.12 = 12%. Blocks reinvestment when breached; never forces extra cuts.
Every cell's full discount gap must close within this many weekly cycles.
Smallest weekly discount step in percentage points. Gaps smaller than this close in one cycle.
The client's own brand as it appears on the platform.
Every spelling of the own brand in the data. Blank = derive from BRAND_NAME. Ingestion fails loud if these match no rows, or match a competitor too.
The quick-commerce platform, e.g. Blinkit.
Hero PRODUCT_IDs that must NEVER be auto-cut, whatever the model says. Separate with |. Write none for no hero protection.
yes = fail loudly if the brand patterns also match a competitor brand (e.g. 'Sun' catching 'Sunfeast'). Keep yes unless you truly own several distinct brand strings.
Cost of goods as a fraction of MRP. 0.50 = 50%. PROXY until finance supplies real per-SKU costs — every profit figure inherits this.
Platform commission as a fraction of selling price. 0.15 = 15%.
Fulfilment cost in Rs per unit.
Train only on the last N days of regular trading. The single biggest accuracy lever — shorter avoids stale price regimes and launch ramps.
Per-cell z-score above which a day is treated as an outlier and excluded from training. 2.0 is the tuned production value; 3.0 keeps more days.
The elbow: deepest discount whose marginal ROI still clears this. 1.0 = the last rupee of discount must at least pay for itself.
Percentile of a cell's own recent discounts treated as its proven-safe floor. 25 = 'we ran this low on a quarter of days and survived'.
Cells with |elasticity| at or below this cannot pay for a discount; they are held/raised, never reinvested. 1.0 is the theorem boundary.
A reinvest candidate must lift volume by at least this %, net of leakage.
A reinvest candidate may sacrifice at most this % of current contribution.
|elasticity| a cell needs before deeper discount is even considered.
Kill-switch: a cell strikes when actual volume misses prediction by more than this %. Two strikes revert the cut.
Prediction error fraction that counts as model drift. 0.15 = 15%.
